groundings.quote: Aristotle follows Socrates and Plato in taking the virtues to be central to a well-lived life.
every ethical virtue is a condition intermediate (a “golden mean” as it is popularly known) between two other states, one involving excess, and the other deficiency
In VII.1–10 Aristotle investigates character traits—continence and incontinence—that are not as blameworthy as the vices but not as praiseworthy as the virtues.
Aristotle wrote two ethical treatises: the Nicomachean Ethics and the Eudemian Ethics.
